_metadata.description: Implement Gem Agent — reads a completed gem.txt and produces tool recommendations, knowledge file specs, and scaffolded knowledge files.
_metadata.instructions: Load tool_evaluation_rules and knowledge_file_patterns. Read the input gem.txt. Score each tool. Identify knowledge files. Write [gemname]_implement.md and scaffold all knowledge files into the same folder.

agent_details.description: Completes the last 10% of Gem deployment: evaluates tools and scaffolds knowledge files based on the gem's instructions.

agent_type.description: Linear: read gem.txt → analyze purpose/task/context → score tools → identify knowledge files → write outputs to same folder.

steps.name: Read Input
steps.description: Read the target [gemname].txt file in full. Parse the four XML sections: <role>, <task>, <constraints>, <context>, <format>.
steps.output: Parsed gem content — role, task, constraints, context, format

steps.name: Analyze Gem Purpose
steps.description: Extract: (a) what the gem's core task is, (b) whether it needs live/current data, (c) whether it produces iterative documents or code, (d) whether it involves visual/audio output, (e) whether it teaches or coaches step-by-step, (f) what domain-specific reference data it needs repeatedly.
steps.output: Analysis summary used to drive tool scoring and knowledge file identification

steps.name: Score Each Tool
steps.description: Apply tool_evaluation_rules to the gem analysis. Assign Enable / Optional / Not Needed to each of the 6 tools with a one-sentence rationale.

steps.name: Identify Knowledge Files
steps.description: Apply knowledge_file_patterns to identify what reference data would improve the gem. For each file: name it, describe its purpose, classify content as generatable (Claude writes it) or user-provided (user must fill it). Maximum 4 knowledge files — prioritize by impact.
steps.output: Knowledge file manifest (name, purpose, content classification per file)

steps.name: Write [gemname]_implement.md
steps.description: Write the implementation guide using the implement_output_template. File goes in the same folder as the input .txt file.
steps.input: Tool recommendation set + knowledge file manifest
steps.output: [gemname]_implement.md in the same folder as input .txt

steps.name: Scaffold Knowledge Files
steps.description: For each knowledge file in the manifest: create the file in the same folder as the input .txt. Generatable files get real content (min 5 example rows/entries). User-provided files get structure + <!-- USER: --> markers. Never create an empty file.
steps.output: One .md file per recommended knowledge file, same folder as input .txt

workflow_based.entry_point: User provides path to a [gemname].txt file
workflow_based.exit_point: [gemname]_implement.md + scaffolded knowledge files in gem_instructions/ folder

inputs.name: gem_txt_path
inputs.format: Relative file path to a completed Gem instructions .txt file

outputs.name: implement_md
outputs.format: Markdown — tool recommendations + knowledge file manifest

outputs.name: knowledge_files
outputs.format: One .md file per recommended knowledge file, same folder as input
outputs.conditions: Produced for each entry in the knowledge file manifest; min 1, max 4

io_contract.side_effects: Creates new files in gem_instructions/ folder only. Never modifies the input .txt or any other existing file.

enable_when: Gem needs real-time or current information (job postings, news, prices, live catalog pages, company info)
Gem instructs users to provide URLs it will then read
Gem needs to verify facts that change over time
optional_when: Gem could benefit from web search occasionally but has stable knowledge files covering most use cases
not_needed_when: Gem operates entirely on user-provided input with no need for external lookups
All reference data is provided via knowledge files and does not change

enable_when: Gem's task requires synthesizing information across many sources (industry reports, academic content, multi-company comparisons)
Gem produces thorough research reports or analyses as its primary output
optional_when: Gem occasionally needs deep research but primarily uses simpler lookups
not_needed_when: Gem only needs quick fact lookups (Google Search is sufficient)
Gem operates on user-provided data without external research

enable_when: Gem produces documents, reports, plans, or code that the user will iterate on collaboratively in the same session
The gem's output format is a structured document (resume, project plan, report)
optional_when: Gem could benefit from canvas for some outputs but primarily produces conversational responses
not_needed_when: Gem produces only conversational text with no structured document output
Gem output is consumed as-is without user editing

enable_when: Gem's core task involves creating or describing visual output (design, illustration, diagrams, art)
Gem is explicitly for visual/creative work
optional_when: Gem could use visuals to support explanations (charts, diagrams) but is primarily text-based
not_needed_when: Gem has no visual output component
Gem's domain is text, data, or audio only

enable_when: Gem's core task involves music creation, composition, or audio production
optional_when: Gem involves creative arts broadly and music could be a relevant output in some conversations
not_needed_when: Gem has no music or audio component

enable_when: Gem's primary role is teaching, tutoring, or coaching users through a learning sequence
Gem uses Socratic questioning, checkpoints, or structured lesson progression
optional_when: Gem explains concepts as part of a larger workflow but is not primarily a tutor
not_needed_when: Gem's workflow is already defined as a step-by-step sequence in its own instructions (the gem handles its own progression)
Gem is primarily an analyst, generator, or advisor rather than a teacher

knowledge_file_patterns.name: [institution]_catalog_index.md
knowledge_file_patterns.use_when: Gem needs to navigate a course catalog or curriculum database for a specific institution
knowledge_file_patterns.content: Table of programs (major/minor/cert) with direct catalog URLs. Prevents the Gem from guessing URL patterns.
knowledge_file_patterns.generation_note: Scaffold the table structure and fill common programs from public knowledge. Mark institution-specific URLs for user verification.

knowledge_file_patterns.name: [domain]_employer_reference.md
knowledge_file_patterns.use_when: Gem recommends companies or employers to users who don't know where to target
knowledge_file_patterns.content: 2-3 companies per sub-industry with hiring reputation notes and typical entry-level role titles
knowledge_file_patterns.generation_note: Generate from public knowledge. Include remote-friendly and regional options where relevant.
knowledge_file_patterns.example_row: | Salesforce | SaaS/CRM | Business Analyst, Sales Development Rep | Strong campus recruiting, BYUI has alumni there |

knowledge_file_patterns.name: [domain]_glossary.md
knowledge_file_patterns.use_when: Gem operates in a specialized domain with jargon the model may not apply consistently
knowledge_file_patterns.content: Term definitions, acronyms, and how the Gem should use or explain each term to the user
knowledge_file_patterns.generation_note: Generate from domain knowledge. Keep to <50 entries.
knowledge_file_patterns.example_row: | Gap Analysis | The process of comparing current state vs. target state to identify what must be bridged | Core methodology of this Gem |

knowledge_file_patterns.name: [task]_rubric.md
knowledge_file_patterns.use_when: Gem scores, evaluates, or grades user submissions against defined criteria
knowledge_file_patterns.content: Scoring dimensions, weight per dimension, what qualifies as low/mid/high for each
knowledge_file_patterns.generation_note: Scaffold dimensions from the gem's task description. User must confirm weights and threshold definitions.
knowledge_file_patterns.example_row: | Technical Skills Match | 40% | <50% match = Low | 50-75% = Medium | >75% = High |

knowledge_file_patterns.name: [process]_guide.md
knowledge_file_patterns.use_when: Gem applies a specific methodology or process the model needs to apply consistently
knowledge_file_patterns.content: Step-by-step process description, decision points, and examples of correct application
knowledge_file_patterns.generation_note: Extract from the gem's <task> and <format> sections and formalize into a reference document.
knowledge_file_patterns.example_row: See Toyota Gap Analysis: Define Current State → Define Target State → Identify Gaps → Prioritize → Close

knowledge_file_patterns.name: [gemname]_example_outputs.md
knowledge_file_patterns.use_when: Gem produces structured reports or analyses and needs anchor examples to maintain consistent format
knowledge_file_patterns.content: 2-3 complete example outputs showing correct format, depth, and tone
knowledge_file_patterns.generation_note: Generate 1 complete example from the gem's few_shot_examples if present. User should add 1-2 real examples from actual usage.
knowledge_file_patterns.example_row: See byui_gap_analyst example: full gap table + next steps for a Marketing junior with no internship experience

sections.heading: # [Gem Name] — Implementation Guide
sections.content: One-sentence summary of what this file covers.

sections.heading: ## Gemini Tools
sections.content: Table: Tool | Recommendation | Rationale. One row per tool. Recommendation = Enable / Optional / Not Needed.

sections.heading: ## Knowledge Files
sections.content: Table: File | Purpose | Status. One row per recommended file. Status = Generated (ready to upload) / Needs User Input (see file for <!-- USER: --> markers).

sections.heading: ## Deployment Checklist
sections.content: Ordered steps: 1. Open Gemini Gem interface. 2. Paste [gemname].txt into Instructions field. 3. Enable/disable tools per table above. 4. Upload knowledge files listed as Generated. 5. Complete user-input sections in flagged files, then upload. 6. Run validation test cases from [gemname].json.

pre_run_checklist: Input [gemname].txt exists and has been QC'd (score >= 80 from make_gem_qc)
implement_gem.json is loaded with tool_evaluation_rules and knowledge_file_patterns
post_run_checklist: [gemname]_implement.md exists in same folder as input .txt
All 6 tools have a recommendation with rationale
At least 1 knowledge file has been scaffolded
No knowledge file is empty — all have real content or <!-- USER: --> markers
No output file was written outside the input .txt folder

test_cases.name: Career/Research Gem
test_cases.expected_output: Google Search = Enable, Deep Research = Optional, Canvas = Optional, Image = Not Needed, Music = Not Needed, Guided Learning = Not Needed. Knowledge files: byui_catalog_index.md + employer_reference.md

test_cases.name: Teaching/Tutoring Gem
test_cases.input: A gem.txt where <role> is a tutor and <task> uses Socratic questioning and checkpoints
test_cases.expected_output: Guided Learning = Enable, Canvas = Enable, Google Search = Optional. Knowledge files: domain glossary + example outputs

test_cases.name: Creative/Design Gem
test_cases.input: A gem.txt where <task> involves creating visual designs or illustrations
test_cases.expected_output: Image Generation = Enable. Music and Guided Learning = Not Needed.

when_to_use: After make_gem_qc scores the .txt at 80 or above
Before configuring the Gem in the Gemini interface
when_not_to_use: On a .txt file that hasn't passed make_gem_qc — fix the instructions first
On a .json file — this agent reads .txt compiled instructions only

changes: Initial version — tool evaluation rules for 6 Gemini tools, 6 knowledge file patterns, workflow output template
